status_line: Monday 7:40 PM ET / 23:40 UTC — twentieth hour. SARAH Run 131 broke the four-sweep production-tree hush with a single net delta in scope: a new SCOUT deliverable, 'Sharpie_Gallery_Research_Momentous_Marks.md' (23,575 B / 22:09 UTC), landed in two non-canonical places at once — both ~/Documents/ and ~/Desktop/CLAUDE/ root — byte-identical (MD5 8a91…28d4). It's the first JARVIS-relevant file ever observed at Desktop/CLAUDE root outside the JARVIS subtree. Logged as new Open Item #19; canonical destination would be output/sharpie-scout-momentous-marks-research-v1.md. Tray now 13 (was 12). Output tree itself stayed zero-delta for the fourth straight push (1,064 files, 48 top-level production). The JARVIS pipeline heartbeat is now confirmed back on its hourly cadence — jarvis-state.json ticked at 21:38 then 22:42 UTC, two consecutive heartbeats after the silent stretch through Runs 128–129. dispatch-inbox.jsonl is silent for a fifth run (still 18:55 UTC) but now decoupled from heartbeat, looking like normal-state. Creative wing dark cycle continues — tenth since the 14:40 MAESTRO/WRITER/GUARDIAN drop. Lands' End is now T-3 days; PARK Gallery deck and T2 v8 still GUARDIAN-unvalidated — urgency lane unchanged. The hf_*.mp4 pair (#7 + #18) is now ~26.4h and ~8.5h stale; Downloads still not mounted (sixth consecutive run), so all four Downloads-resident Open Items (#7, #9, #15, #18) are assumed unchanged but not byte-verified. New SCOUT file (#19) is the highest-leverage one-line prompt this hour, displacing the Higgsfield bulk-decision. Repo still 8 commits behind origin locally with .git/index.lock pinned; writable-clone workaround stays in play.
